name: I think the loud machines at work damaged my hearing but symptoms started months later, is it too late?

mainEntity.name: I think the loud machines at work damaged my hearing but symptoms started months later, is it too late?

acceptedAnswer.text: File within 1 year of last exposure in Utah; get audiogram and evidence quickly.[3]
acceptedAnswer.description: No, it's not too late if within the statute of limitations; in Utah, you have 1 year from the last injurious noise exposure for occupational hearing loss claims.[3] Context: Gradual noise-induced hearing loss (NIHL) triggers the clock from your final significant exposure, not symptom onset.[3][4] Key factors: Proof of ongoing workplace noise, audiometric tests showing NIHL pattern, and no non-work causes like age.[1][4] Practical guidance: Document everything now—get a hearing test, gather coworker statements, and file promptly with the Utah Labor Commission or Idaho Industrial Commission; a lawyer can extend via appeals.[3][7] Early action preserves benefits like hearing aids and lost wages.

description: File within 1 year of last exposure in Utah; get audiogram and evidence quickly.[3]
